content: The recommended protein intake for teenage boys is between 0.5 to 1 gram of protein per pound of body weight, or 1 to 2 grams per kilogram of body weight.
Eating a mix of foods is essential to get all vitamins and minerals and minerals, focusing on protein intake first.
There is no cap on the number of eggs a teenage boy can eat per day, as long as he is willing to eat them.
Protein powder is safe for teenage boys, but not recommended as a main source of protein.

Using legumes as a major staple in the diet will help get you to a good place with vitamins and minerals, and towards a higher protein than you would get with other carby foods and with cheap junk food.
